reviews.comment:
Excellent job and customer service. My wife and I have had both of our cars tinted with Frederick Window Tinting. Both times I have texted the day before and both times they responded immediately and were able to squeeze me in with no problems. I have had vehicles tinted in the past and have found that you get what you pay for. And I can tell you from experience FWT provides a service and product that is worth every penny.

Very friendly family owned business, I would highly recommend to all my friends and family. Joe and Sharon are the best in town.

reviewReply.comment: Thank you for your loyalty! We appreciate your business!

reviews.comment:
Joe and Sharyn are great! I first got my 2018 Honda Civic Hatchback tinted by them because I learned that most tint shops have to remove the spoiler to tint my car. So I searched and found them on Facebook and learned that they bought a spare rear window to make the tinting process easier and not have to remove the spoiler.

They did a fantastic job with the tint, so great that I brought in my second car that I had just purchased for them to tint and the job came out great too. I highly recommend them and will have all my future vehicles tinted by them.

reviewReply.comment: We appreciate your repeat business, Heber! Thank you for taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.comment: Excellent and caring staff that are highly professional. Did an fantastic job matching the tint on my truck at a fair price without trying to upsell me like others often will and worked to accommodate my schedule. Absolutely recommend
reviews.name: accounts/114723450049664810853/locations/10123051285004451502/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSURRbGFISkpnEAE

reviews.comment:
Egotistical and arrogant couple who frankly don't stand by his bad work. Found imperfections on every single window, and the wife struts around the car trying to tell me all but one are just dust and that as long as I can't see them from 3ft out I'm basically just being a pain. I could see most from 10ft out, and I think they knew he did a bad job because they went straight to offering a refund instead of fixing anything. The day to remove comes and he literally rips all the tint off WITHOUT HEAT and leaves glue all over the place, and the wife tries to 'help' ripping then gives up and walks away. The kicker was when he challenged me to bring it to a place that can do a better job and come back for him inspect it; then if it was superior he would buy me lunch (though I overheard him telling her he'd pay for the new tint, but the wife tells him no and to just offer lunch). Caveat emptor!

Follow-up response:
It's sad that a so-called "reputable" tint shop is claiming they don't know how to check for prior tinting, and somehow tried to make it my fault. We're the second owner of the car (spoke with dealer), and it has NOT been tinted before, contrary to the story you're telling. So any adhesive left over was not there prior to your substandard removal, and was the reason we had to go back at the second place at all. After he spent time to remove all the adhesive you left, there was still some left over that cured under the new tint. If we went back it was only because we spent $400 for a "quality tint" only to look at dust spots, creases and bad lines when I could have paid half of that and gone somewhere else. The only reality is that sometimes you don't get what you pay for, and if you actually care about your car you get a condescending owner looking down their nose at you.

You're right about one thing, this wasn't worth the time I already took. I wanted others to know the REALITY of the work, and if they actually look at their car and care about it's appearance they'll be cautious around a business built on lying and subterfuge.

reviewReply.comment: It's regrettable that you and your girlfriend had such a terrible experience with us. When the vehicle was first brought to us, none of us were aware that the vehicle had been previously tinted. If you, your girlfriend or we were aware that there was leftover adhesive, there would have be a charge to remove it prior to tinting the vehicle. The minor amounts of adhesive left behind were no doubt the cause for the imperfections you found. We were absolutely willing to retint the entire vehicle providing you pay for the adhesive removal from the previous tint job, to which she declined. Per the text correspondence your girlfriend chose the option to receive a full refund and have our film removed. We made her whole and returned the vehicle the way it was initially brought to us. Within the first few weeks of a tint job our film can be removed with minimal to no adhesive.  We have found that over the years it is generally best to let customers find another shop when their expectations exceed reality. This theory was confirmed when hearing about the multiple visits that had to be made to the other shop that tinted your vehicle after us to acquire an acceptable tint job. Qui mos non semper rectam! Follow up response. As the second owner of the vehicle, it is impossible for you to make the claim that it was never tinted. You purchased a used vehicle. People tint their vehicle and have the film removed prior to trading in all the time, so a dealership would have no idea unless they themselves did the removal. The adhesive is clear and therefore practically invisible. When window film is applied over top of  adhesive it becomes very visible when the film dries. It's nearly impossible to see until the film dries. This further proves why you, the other tinter and myself didn't see it until a day or so later after the film dried resulting in multiple visits. I fully admit that I missed the glue when tinting your vehicle. I made an assumption that you bought the vehicle new, not used. 100% my fault. However, if I did find the glue prior to tinting your vehicle, there would have been an additional charge to remove it. That same charge was what we asked for when we offered to retint the vehicle. In the end, we made you whole with a full refund no questions asked. We removed our film and returned your vehicle in the same state it was when it was first brought to us. At the end of the day, we do everything we can to satisfy each and every person who comes to our shop, but sometimes this just simply can't be achieved. We're happy that you were able to find someone who could make you happy. All the best, Joe and Sharyn!

reviews.name: accounts/114723450049664810853/locations/10123051285004451502/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNBZzhpTnNnRRAB

reviews.comment:
What can you say about a local family owned and operated Window Tinting business that takes absolute pride in the work they do and customer service they provide. The moment you walk in you're treated just like family. They were very accommodating to my quick turnaround need to have some old (cheap) tint removed from a recently purchased vehicle that failed MD State Inspection due to the 3rd brake light being covered by the rear window tint.

Frustrated and concerned about the potential damage that could occur with improper tint removal, I quickly gave FWT a call which I trust 100%. Sharyn picked up and provided me an appointment that worked perfectly into my crazy schedule.

Brought the vehicle by the shop and Joe went to work on it. Certainly not an easy job due to the poor quality of the tint used by the previous owner but he made it look easy just like a true professional would do. Once it was off the car the tint was noticeably purple (garbage) so the failed MD inspection was a blessing in disguise. Can't wait to bring the car back in 2 weeks for some top of the line ceramic tint!

In conclusion, it's remarkable the speed, care and quality of work they provide. I don't know why you'd go anywhere else when you have such a great place right here in Frederick that gives such great service, uses top notch materials and industry competitive pricing.

Highly recommended!

reviewReply.comment: Thank you so much for your wonderful review!
